title: Code-grounding evidence contract
description: The normalized three-valued evidence model emitted by the code-grounding oracle (epic 8f6c). Every probe returns ONE of these: `refuted`/`match` (resolved) or `abstain` (with a CLOSED structured reason). Match and abstain share this one shape. SARIF 2.1.0 is the interchange format at the edges; this model is a superset (SARIF has no native refuted/abstain/coverage). Authored as the single source of truth for the contract; validated directly via schemas.validator('grounding'), not wired to a command --output.

abstain_reason.description: CLOSED structured abstention reason (SMT `unknown`-with-reason model). No open `…`: `version_skew` (the #1 real failure) and `invalid_detector` (a project detector failing validation) are first-class; `other` is the explicit catch-all.

outcome.description: The three-valued, confirm-only outcome. `refuted` = an asserted absence was DISPROVED (resolution lane; never an asserted absence). `match` = a detector matched (Engine B). `abstain` = fail-open skip with a recorded `reason`.

job.description: Which of the three oracle jobs produced this evidence. `refute` = resolution lane (disprove an asserted absence). `applies` = self-declared applicability detection. `smell` = low-threshold attention-router scan.

provenance_tier.description: Provenance tier of the resolution. T0 = manifest/registry existence; T1 = universal-ctags repo-wide symbol/file existence; T2 = LSP/SCIP semantic (deferred to epic 850f, abstain-by-default in v1).

coverage.description: The coverage record for ONE backend invocation. The visible skip IS the coverage record — a `skipped` status with a `reason` is how a fail-open skip is reconstructed; never a silent no-op.

backend.description: The backend that produced (or would have produced) this evidence, e.g. `ctags`, `opengrep`, `ast-grep`, `lizard`, `tree-sitter`, `registry`.

status.description: `ran` = the backend executed to completion. `skipped` = a fail-open condition tripped (the `reason` says which).

version.description: The pinned/recorded backend version (version skew is the #1 real failure, so it is recorded).

reason.description: Set iff status=skipped: why the backend was skipped (same enum as the evidence-level abstain reason).

reference.description: An EXPLICIT, structured reference checked by the resolution lane (reference-in contract — the oracle does NOT extract references from prose). The review agent emits these; the oracle verifies them. Fleshed out further by Engine A (story 1532); kept permissive here.

kind.description: What class of name is being checked: a module import, a code symbol (function/class/etc.), a package dependency (routes to the T0 deps lane), a file path, or a member/attribute (dotted recv.attr — T1-abstain, T2 territory).

name.description: The bare name asserted absent/invalid (the high-base-rate hallucination class at T1).

language.description: Optional language hint for the reference.

location.description: Where the evidence points in the repo (Engine B match, or a refuted reference's definition site). Null for evidence with no single site (e.g. a dependency existence check).

line_end.description: 1-based end line; 0/omitted means through end of file.

evidence.description: ONE normalized evidence record. The single internal finding/evidence shape across all backends and all three jobs; match and abstain share it.

reason.description: REQUIRED non-null iff outcome=abstain (the structured abstention reason); MUST be null for outcome=refuted/match.

detector_id.description: The (namespaced) detector id that produced this evidence (Engine B); null on the resolution lane.

reference.description: The structured reference this evidence is about (resolution lane); null for detector matches.

attention_only.description: True for recall-oriented attention routers (smell scans) that FLAG an area for an LLM to inspect — never blocking.

detail.description: Optional human-readable context (e.g. the message for a detector match, or the failing-tool detail for an abstain).

coverage_report.description: A roll-up of evidence + the per-backend coverage so a caller can report how much of the query was actually answered vs skipped.
